_comment: Golden set built from Discogs / Bandcamp / DeathTechno verification. For relevant/false_friend entries the convention is artist-only matching: the runner's _same_artist token comparison matches any track by that artist in the production output. Specific track titles are only used in seed queries (where a real seed track is required to drive /similar).
_metric: nDCG@10 with grades: relevant=2, false_friend=0, unmarked=1
_validation_notes: Seed tracks were verified to exist on Discogs/Bandcamp (April 2026 search).

Relevant artist lists are based on label-mate relationships and explicit DJ-support data (e.g. 'Linear System supported by Luke Slater, Ben Sims, Mulero, Rødhåd, Shifted, Sigha, Ben Klock, Marcel Dettmann' — DeathTechno artist page).
False_friends are deliberately picked as peak-time / festival-techno artists who would superficially share the 'techno' tag but represent a clearly different stylistic register.
Charlotte de Witte appears as a control seed: when she's the seed, peak-time peers SHOULD surface; when other seeds are queried, she should NOT surface.

seeds.style_notes: Pole Group classic, hypnotic techno, ~134 BPM. Title track from the 2011 EP on Pole Group. Defining example of the Spanish hypnotic school — long-form, dark, deep.

relevant.reason: Pole Group co-founder, similar production aesthetic, frequent collaborator with Mulero

seeds.style_notes: UK/Portugal-based, hypnotic techno with rolling grooves and high BPMs (~135-140). Released on Sk_Eleven (2021). Stylistic peers include Mote Evolver / Klockworks school, supported by Rødhåd, DVS1, Blawan.

seeds.style_notes: Spanish project (since 2020), Costa Rica-based. Hypnotic techno with strong basslines, cold atmospheres, shamanic rhythms. Releases on Edit Select, Dynamic Reflection, Planet Rhythm, Symbolism, Modularz, Molecular Recordings. Track from Molecular 028 (2022).

seeds.style_notes: Berlin-based DJ/producer, originally Amsterdam; owner of Somov Records. Hypnotic techno with organic textures and 'oceanic' atmospheres. Veil is the opener of SMV001 (2020), the label's first release. Also affiliated with Sk_Eleven (Anahata EP).

relevant.reason: Direct connection: Rene Wise remixed SMV004 for Somov Records (2021)

seeds.style_notes: CONTROL SEED — peak-time techno, KNTXT label, ~140-145 BPM. Track from Apollo EP (KNTXT017). Used to verify the system: (a) does NOT recommend hypnotic peers when seed is peak-time, AND (b) DOES recommend peak-time peers when seed IS peak-time. Mirror test of the false_friends used in hypnotic seeds.
